title: Members: COVID-19 Testing and Screening Expert Advisory Panel - Canada.ca
content:
## COVID-19 Testing and Screening Expert Advisory Panel: Members
- Reports and summaries
The diverse expertise of members of the Panel ensured a wide range of  perspectives and advice on testing and screening for COVID-19. The advisory  committee comprised 13 individuals with expertise in epidemiology, virology,  advanced data analytics, pediatrics, health care provision and technology  assessment, both internationally and in Canada. This list describes the  members’ roles at the time of their service on the Panel.
Sue Paish (co-chair)
- CEO of Canada’s Digital Technology Supercluster
Dr. Irfan Dhalla (co-chair)
- Vice President, Physician Quality, Unity Health Toronto
- Clinician Investigator and Associate Professor, Infectious Diseases, University of Toronto
- President and CEO, Canadian Foundation for Healthcare Improvement
- President and CEO, Alberta Health Services
- Medical Director, BC Centre for Disease Control Public Health Laboratory
- Co-Chair of Canada’s the Immunity Task Force and Professor of Medicine
- Professor and Associate Dean, Faculty of Medicine, Memorial University
- Infectious Disease Specialist, CHU de Québec-Université Laval
- CEO Wellesley Institute
- Medical Officer of Health, Kingston, Frontenac, Lennox & Addington Public Health
- Professor, Queen’s University, and Ontario Research Chair in Bioethics
- Dr. Mona Nemer, Chief Science Advisor;
- Dr. Stephen Lucas, Deputy Minister for Health Canada;
- Iain Stewart, President of the Public Health Agency of Canada;
- Simon Kennedy, Deputy Minister of Innovation, Science and Economic Development;
- Mitch Davies, President of the National Research Council
- Valerie Gideon, Associate Deputy Minister, Indigenous Services Canada
- Dr. Howard Njoo, Deputy Chief Public Health Officer, Public Health Agency of Canada
- Dr. Guillaume Poliquin, A/Scientific Director General, National Microbiology Laboratory, Public Health Agency of Canada
